---
title: "About"
---

I'm a GIS and software developer moving into spatial epidemiology. My background
is in building geospatial software; my current focus is applying that to
public-health problems — surveillance data, environmental exposure, and the
spatial and temporal structure that ordinary regression tends to ignore.

## Now

I'm finishing an MPH in Epidemiology at the University of Memphis. My thesis is
quantitative: a study of West Nile virus mosquito surveillance across California,
looking at how environmental covariates, surveillance effort, and space-time
patterns relate to infection rates in mosquito pools. The modeling side leans on
Bayesian hierarchical and spatiotemporal methods.

Professionally, I develop GIS and risk-modeling software in support of FEMA's
Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation program. Day to day that means C#, C++, and
SQL Server work against ArcGIS-based data and modeling workflows — database
updates, hazard and loss logic, and the validation that goes with changing a
model people rely on.

## How I work

A few things I keep coming back to:

- **Treat surveillance as data with a process behind it.** Where and how hard you
  look shapes what you find. Detection and effort belong in the model, not in a
  footnote.
- **Validate against something.** New spatial code gets checked against
  established implementations (PySAL, R packages, published results) before I
  trust its output.
- **Make it reproducible.** Pipelines over one-off scripts; version control;
  environments that someone else can rebuild.
- **Say what the analysis can and can't support.** I'd rather state a limitation
  plainly than round a result up.

## Background and interests

My work sits across spatial epidemiology, environmental health, public-health
GIS, and applied statistics. I'm particularly interested in vector-borne disease
surveillance, spatial cluster detection, and the methods problems that come up
when surveillance data is sparse, uneven, or collected for something other than
research.

Outside the health work, I like building desktop and geospatial tools that are
fast and that people can actually run on their own machines.

## Contact

- Email: [minorsecond@gmail.com](mailto:minorsecond@gmail.com)
- GitHub: [github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond)
- [Curriculum vitae](cv.qmd)
